explicit_text: I am a down home girl from the South, born and raised in the southern part of Atlanta, Georgia. The South is deeply ancestral for me, as my family includes preachers, medicine people, and midwives from this region. Even though I moved around, the South always felt like home and a spiritual portal. When the COVID-19 pandemic started, I felt the South calling me back, especially to reconnect with the land and my roots.

cues: born and raised in South of Atlanta

explicit_text: My best friend Sarah McKeever once told me that the South is a portal, and that really resonated with me. Her words helped me understand the deep spiritual connection I have with the South and why this place feels like a gateway to different timelines and worlds. Sarah's perspective has influenced how I view my work and my relationship with the land and my ancestors.

explicit_text: I was born and raised in the southern part of Atlanta, Georgia, which is where my roots and family history are deeply planted. Growing up there, I was surrounded by a lineage of preachers and medicine people, which shaped my spiritual work today. Atlanta represents the beginning of my journey before I moved to other places like Orlando and Dallas.

Even though I have moved away, Georgia remains an important part of my identity and connection to the South.

explicit_text: After getting married, my husband and I moved to Orlando, Florida, which was a significant step in our journey as a family. It was one of the places we lived before settling in Dallas and eventually moving back to the South. Orlando was part of the transitional phase in my life as I sought to find a place that truly felt like home.

explicit_text: We lived in Dallas, Texas, when the COVID-19 pandemic began, which was a very uncertain and fearful time for my family. We had just closed on our first home there and were expecting our second child. The pandemic made us feel disconnected from the land, and it was during this time that I felt the South calling me back to find peace and reconnect with my ancestral roots.

We had just closed on our first home there and were expecting our second child. The pandemic made us feel disconnected from the land, and it was during this time that I felt the southern United States calling me back to find peace and reconnect with my ancestral roots.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic was a dark and uncertain time for me and my family, especially since I was nearly three months pregnant when the world shut down. It forced me to reach out to my elders and ancestors for guidance and strength, learning about survival through their stories. My daughter was born at home in October 2020, and I called on the power of my ancestors to help me bring her safely into a world filled with fear and sickness.

explicit_text: Though I had never lived in Charlotte before, when we started looking for a new home in the South, Charlotte felt familiar and welcoming. Upon moving here, I discovered I had ancestors who lived in the Carolinas, which deepened my connection to this place. The energy of the land here, especially the vibrant trees and water spirits, has been a powerful part of my spiritual journey and healing.

explicit_text: North Carolina has become my new home, a place I feel spiritually connected to even though I’m still new here. I’ve learned that my ancestors lived here, which makes this land feel like it was calling me all along. I’ve been making offerings and agreements with the land and water spirits to live in harmony and heal alongside nature in this beautiful part of the South.

explicit_text: Mother Earth holds us and all the elements support our lives, which is something I deeply respect and honor in my spiritual work. I believe it’s important to be mindful of her and to make peaceful agreements with the land and nature. One night, I asked Mother Earth if she welcomed us on the land where we now raise our family, and shortly after, a family of ducks appeared, which I took as a sign of her acceptance.

explicit_text: We first heard about the virus in Wuhan just as we had settled into our new home in Dallas. At that moment, no one had answers, and fear and uncertainty were everywhere. Hearing about Wuhan marked the beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic for us and set the stage for the challenging months that followed during my pregnancy.
implicit_text: We first heard about the virus in a Chinese city just as we had settled into our new home in Dallas. At that moment, no one had answers, and fear and uncertainty were everywhere. Hearing about that city marked the beginning of the global pandemic for us and set the stage for the challenging months that followed during my pregnancy.

explicit_text: During the COVID-19 pandemic, I learned from my grandmother that her mother was born during the 1918 flu pandemic. This connection to a past pandemic baby gave me strength and hope, reminding me that my family has survived such crises before. It helped me see that although the current times were difficult, they were not the end, and survival is in my blood.
